insights.title: Pan parameter has zero audible effect without modulators in the Pan chain
insights.summary: The Pan parameter defines the maximum range of pan modulation, not a static pan position. If the Pan Modulation chain is empty, applyEffect() exits immediately and no panning is applied regardless of the Pan knob value.

insights.title: Width above 100% can introduce out-of-phase content
insights.summary: Setting Width beyond 100% exaggerates the side signal and can produce content where left and right channels are partially out of phase. This may cause partial cancellation when the output is summed to mono.

insights.title: Width has no effect on a mono source (L==R)
insights.summary: Mid/side width processing scales the side signal (R-L). If both channels carry the same signal, the side is zero and Width has no audible effect regardless of the knob position.

insights.title: Use Haas effect or chorus to widen mono samples instead of StereoFX Width alone
insights.summary: When widening a mono source, StereoFX Width alone does nothing because there is no stereo difference to expand. Community practice is to create stereo differences first using a short delay (Haas effect ~30ms on one channel) or a chorus node, then optionally follow with StereoFX Width.

insights.title: Stereo plugin sums to mono in FL Studio when HI_SUPPORT_MONO_TO_STEREO=1 is set manually
insights.summary: Using the HI_SUPPORT_MONO_TO_STEREO=1 extra definition together with the SupportMonoFX project checkbox causes stereo-to-mono collapse in FL Studio VST3 builds. The fix is to remove the manual preprocessor definition and rely solely on the SupportMonoFX project setting, or use ForceStereoOutput instead.
insights.verify_hint: grep HI_SUPPORT_MONO_TO_STEREO and HI_SUPPORT_MONO_CHANNEL_LAYOUT in HISE plugin wrapper source; check if project settings override extra definitions

insights.title: Use ForceStereoOutput + SupportMonoFX for cross-DAW mono-to-stereo compatibility
insights.summary: To get a mono-to-stereo FX plugin that works correctly across FL Studio, Logic, and Reaper without manual per-format build flag changes, enable ForceStereoOutput in project settings and check SupportMonoFX. Avoid setting HI_SUPPORT_MONO_TO_STEREO manually in Extra Definitions.

insights.title: Scriptnode mid/side network as alternative for modulation-driven stereo width
insights.summary: Users needing per-voice or modulation-driven width control implement their own mid/side processing in a scriptnode DspNetwork. The StereoFX Width parameter is static (no modulation chain), so scriptnode is the recommended path when dynamic width is required.
